2014-09-01.title: NATO Set to Ratify Pledge on Joint Defense in Case of Major Cyberattack
2014-09-01.abstract: For the first time, a cyberattack on any of the 28 NATO nations could be declared an attack on all of them, much like a ground invasion or an airborne bombing.

2014-09-01.title: Lifting the Soul, and the Spanish Economy, Too
2014-09-01.abstract: Despite the sagging Spanish economy — or perhaps because of it — the pilgrimage to the shrine of St. James in northwestern Galicia has become a booming part of modern Spain’s tourism industry.

2014-09-01.title: Ukrainian Woman, Held Up to Public Abuse, Is Released
2014-09-01.abstract: Irina Dovgan was wrapped in a Ukrainian flag and forced to stand on a sidewalk while holding a sign that read “She kills our children,” as passers-by slapped, kicked and spit on her.

2014-09-01.title: Redrawing a State in India Drives Land Prices to the Sky
2014-09-01.abstract: In the newly divided state of Andhra Pradesh, real estate speculation has overtaken villages that may become part of a sprawling new capital.

2014-09-01.title: Germany Steps Up Its Response to Global Security Crises
2014-09-01.abstract: The country’s top politicians on Sunday approved the delivery of thousands of machine guns and hand grenades, as well as hundreds of antitank missiles, to Kurdish forces battling Islamic militants in Iraq.

2014-09-02.title: Trying to Rebuild a Kurdish Defense Force
2014-09-02.abstract: When ISIS militants rolled into Kurdistan in August, the collapse of the pesh merga left tens of thousands of defenseless residents in ISIS’ path.

2014-09-02.title: Tarnishing a Reputation as Storied Warriors
2014-09-02.abstract: The Kurdish pesh merga collapse left tens of thousands of defenseless residents in ISIS’ path, further calling into question fundamental assumptions about Iraq’s security.

2014-09-02.title: Billboard Drives Home Extent of Corruption as Schools Suffer
2014-09-02.abstract: In Mexico, which despite a healthy education budget has dilapidated schools, a billboard shows how much money goes to no-show teachers who collect pay without working.

2014-09-02.title: Years of Rape and ‘Utter Contempt’ in Britain
2014-09-02.abstract: In Rotherham, England, at least 1,400 children were lured into a network of sexual exploitation while the authorities looked the other way.

2014-09-02.title: Militants Add Cluster Bombing to Tactics, Rights Group Says
2014-09-02.abstract: Human Rights Watch said there was evidence that the extremist group used the munitions in July and August as it fought in northern Syria.

2014-09-03.title: India: Militants Are Killed in Shootout in Kashmir
2014-09-03.abstract: The Indian authorities said they killed three antigovernment militants Tuesday after a shootout in the Indian-administered region of Kashmir.

2014-09-03.title: Israel Says Hamas Is Hurt Significantly
2014-09-03.abstract: In a recap of the 50-day war in Gaza, Israel says that it killed several hundred militants, and that militant groups operating in Gaza still have as many as 3,000 rockets on hand.

2014-09-03.title: Ukraine, Caught in a Vise
2014-09-03.abstract: Behind the continuing military conflict in eastern Ukraine lies an economic split. The country is moving toward Europe, straining its longstanding ties with Russia.

2014-09-03.title: U.S. and Europe Are Struggling With Response to a Bold Russia
2014-09-03.abstract: Officials hope that any new measures will have more impact than the economic sanctions imposed on Moscow so far.

2014-09-03.title: Absurd or Astute: Brazil’s Political Ads
2014-09-03.abstract: To capture attention and votes in Brazil’s fall elections, candidates use free allotted airtime to release outrageous and provocative ads.

2014-09-04.title: Australian Leader Seeks Deal to Sell Uranium to India
2014-09-04.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Abbott said ahead of a visit to India that a uranium sale deal would help support “the world’s emerging democratic superpower.”

2014-09-04.title: Former First Lady of France Colors In Details of an Affair
2014-09-04.abstract: Valérie Trierweiler, France’s former unofficial first lady, has written a book describing her version of events that led to her separation with President François Hollande.

2014-09-04.title: To Lure Tourists, a Remote Village in Spain Turns Its Eye to the Arts
2014-09-04.abstract: An art festival has sprouted up to support Spanish artists in hopes of also drawing tourists to the remote village of Genalguacil.

2014-09-04.title: Steven Sotloff, U.S. Hostage Slain by ISIS, Was Also a Citizen of Israel
2014-09-04.abstract: The Jewish roots and Israeli past of Steven J. Sotloff, a journalist from Miami, were kept hidden until hope for his survival was no longer possible.

2014-09-04.title: Cuts at W.H.O. Hurt Response to Ebola Crisis
2014-09-04.abstract: The epidemic has exposed gaping holes in the ability to tackle outbreaks in an increasingly interconnected world, where diseases can quickly spread from remote villages to cities housing millions of people.

2014-09-05.title: Jailed for Protests, Activists in Egypt and Bahrain Turn to Hunger Strikes
2014-09-05.abstract: Three years after they helped lead street protests, prominent Arab Spring activists in two nations are starving themselves in prison.

2014-09-05.title: As Islamist Militants Advance, Residents Flee a Nigerian City
2014-09-05.abstract: Many people were fearful that the army could not repel an attack on Maiduguri by the Islamist militants, who have taken over other cities in the region.

2014-09-05.title: Hong Kong’s Democrats Clamor for Spot on Crowded U.S. Agenda
2014-09-05.abstract: The United States is sending Susan E. Rice, the national security adviser, to China this weekend, to meet with Chinese leaders, where she will discuss Hong Kong, the near collision of a Navy surveillance plane and a Chinese jet and nuclear talks with Iran.

2014-09-05.title: On Ukraine, the West Sidesteps a Fraught Term
2014-09-05.abstract: After four months of fighting in eastern Ukraine, few countries have chosen the word “invasion” to describe the slow-burning war.

2014-09-05.title: Israel Documents on Hamas Role in Killings of Teenagers
2014-09-05.abstract: The documents relate to an investigation and indictment of the man suspected of leading the kidnappers, and provide no evidence that the top leaders of Hamas directed or had prior knowledge of the plot to abduct the three Israeli youths.

2014-09-06.title: Since Nazi Occupation, a Fist Raised in Resistance
2014-09-06.abstract: In 1941, Manolis Glezos and a friend ripped the Nazi flag down from the Acropolis in Athens. Now 92, he continues to take to the ramparts as a leftist member of the European Parliament.

2014-09-06.title: Indian Leader’s Talk to Schoolchildren Draws Fire
2014-09-06.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who rarely speaks to reporters, answered children’s questions on Friday as part of a nationwide broadcast to mark Teacher’s Day. His opponents said the event highlighted the heavy-handedness of his government.

2014-09-06.title: Iran Fails to Address All Nuclear Concerns, U.N. Says
2014-09-06.abstract: Prospects for an agreement over Iran’s nuclear program appeared to suffer a setback when a United Nations monitoring agency said the Iranians had yet to meet two measures to ensure that their activities are peaceful.

2014-09-06.title: Rare Outbreak of Dengue Fever in Japan
2014-09-06.abstract: Japan’s health minister on Friday urged the public not to panic after a popular park in central Tokyo was closed because of a rare outbreak of dengue fever.

2014-09-06.title: Toronto Mayor Called to Testify in Canada
2014-09-06.abstract: Rob Ford, the mayor of Toronto, told reporters on Friday that he had been subpoenaed to testify at a court hearing related to efforts by his unofficial driver to recover a video showing the mayor smoking crack cocaine.

2014-09-07.title: Building Good Will in a Troubled Afghan District, but Not Without a Fight
2014-09-07.abstract: A day trip to a troubled district with America’s elite troops and Afghan government and military officials was characterized by the absurd and unexpected, features of the war in the country.

2014-09-07.title: Wealthy Chinese Travelers Lining Up to Blast Off
2014-09-07.abstract: China is set to become the world’s largest market for the fledgling space tourism industry.

2014-09-07.title: North Korea Says It Will Put American on Trial
2014-09-07.abstract: Matthew Todd Miller is accused of shredding his tourist visa and demanding asylum, which the North says is a “hostile act” against the country.

2014-09-07.title: U.S. Launches New Airstrikes on ISIS to Protect Dam in Iraq
2014-09-07.abstract: The airstrikes were aimed at stopping militants from seizing the Haditha Dam and preventing the possibility of floodwaters flowing toward Baghdad.

2014-09-07.title: 1889: Research May Help Prolong Women’s Fertility
2014-09-07.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: A doctor in Paris worked on prolonging fertility in 1889.

2014-09-08.title: Ukraine and Russia Face Gaps in a Truce and a Chasm on the Issues
2014-09-08.abstract: Both sides agreed to a “decentralization of power” in Ukraine in the terms of a truce agreement, but just what form that may take remains one issue of contention.

2014-09-08.title: Despite Sanctions, Cash Keeps Flowing at Playground for Russia’s Rich
2014-09-08.abstract: Business owners in seaside resort towns like Italy’s Forte dei Marmi worry that further sanctions could hurt business, but so far the reach of big Russian money seems little affected.

2014-09-08.title: Palestinian Leader Assails Hamas, Calling Unity Pact Into Question
2014-09-08.abstract: At an Arab League gathering, President Mahmoud Abbas accused Islamists of running a shadow government in Gaza despite a reconciliation pact with his Fatah faction.

2014-09-08.title: Destroying ISIS May Take Years, U.S. Officials Say
2014-09-08.abstract: The Obama administration is preparing to carry out a phased campaign requiring a sustained effort that could last until after President Obama has left office, according to officials.

2014-09-08.title: Qatar’s Support of Islamists Alienates Allies Near and Far
2014-09-08.abstract: Qatar has helped support a spectrum of Islamist groups by providing safe haven, diplomatic mediation, financial aid and, in certain instances, weapons.

2014-09-09.title: Coalition Urges Nations to Decriminalize Drugs and Drug Use
2014-09-09.abstract: The Global Commission on Drug Policy, a group made up of former presidents and politicians, is calling for less emphasis on punishment and more on treatment while still targeting criminal drug gang operations.

2014-09-09.title: Ebola Cases Reported Up Sharply in Liberia
2014-09-09.abstract: The World Health Organization said the number of new patients in Liberia pointed to “a large but previously invisible caseload.”

2014-09-09.title: They Built It. People Came. Now They Go.
2014-09-09.abstract: A government program to solve Mexico’s chronic housing shortage produced huge developments without jobs or supporting services, forcing thousands of new owners to abandon their homes.

2014-09-09.title: U.S. Delegation Is to Visit Moscow to Review Claims of a Missile Violation
2014-09-09.abstract: United States officials believe Russia may have tested a ground-launched cruise missile in violation of the 1987 Soviet-American treaty.

2014-09-09.title: A President Whose Assurances Have Come Back to Haunt Him
2014-09-09.abstract: President Obama has been undermined by his own statements, like the one where he said Iraq was “sovereign, stable and self-reliant” with “a representative government.”

2014-09-10.title: Ukraine Town Bears Scars of Russian Offensive That Turned Tide in Conflict
2014-09-10.abstract: Days after the Russian artillery units’ assault, Ukraine agreed to a cease-fire cementing the rebels’ hold on its southeast region.

2014-09-10.title: Canada Chases Down an Arctic Mystery, and Some See a Political Strategy
2014-09-10.abstract: Canadian researchers say they have located a ship from Sir John Franklin’s disastrous expedition in the 1840s to map the Northwest Passage, an area of new international interest as Arctic ice retreats.

2014-09-10.title: Fiji Government Says Its Golan Heights Peacekeepers Will Be Released Soon
2014-09-10.abstract: The country’s 45-member United Nations peacekeeping contingent in the Golan Heights was seized by the Syrian branch of Al Qaeda nearly two weeks ago.

2014-09-10.title: Amid Intensifying Requests, American Military Aid to Ukraine Stalls
2014-09-10.abstract: Despite appeals from the Ukrainian armed forces, the United States has provided only a modest package of nonlethal assistance to the government in Kiev, and much of it has yet to arrive.

2014-09-10.title: Violence Erupts in Yemen Capital After Weeks of Rallies
2014-09-10.abstract: Pro-government forces in the capital opened fire on Tuesday at thousands of demonstrators from the Shiite religious minority, killing six and injuring dozens, officials and witnesses said.

2014-09-11.title: Human Rights Abuses Reported in Junta-Ruled Thailand
2014-09-11.abstract: Amnesty International cites at least one report of torture and hundreds having to report to the military, as well as heavy-handed restrictions on political expression.

2014-09-11.title: Mexico: Discovery of Coca Plants Puzzles Authorities
2014-09-11.abstract: The authorities discovered and destroyed more than 1,600 coca plants this week near the Guatemala border, the first such find in Mexico in recent memory.

2014-09-11.title: New Military Campaign Extends a Legacy of War
2014-09-11.abstract: President Obama on Wednesday night may have ensured that he will pass his successor a volatile and incomplete war, just as he inherited one when he took office.

2014-09-11.title: Speech Excerpt: Obama on ISIS Strategy
2014-09-11.abstract: The president said the United States would lead a coalition against ISIS and laid out the four parts of the strategy.

2014-09-11.title: Struggling to Gauge ISIS Threat, Even as U.S. Prepares to Act
2014-09-11.abstract: As President Obama prepares to send the United States on what could be a yearslong military campaign, American intelligence agencies have concluded that the group poses no immediate threat to the United States.

2014-09-12.title: Beleaguered Pakistani Capital Inches Back to Normal
2014-09-12.abstract: Islamabad, which has been disrupted by vast traffic jams, barricaded streets and teeming political rallies, is slowly beginning to inch back to its usual routines.

2014-09-12.title: With Eye on Scotland, Catalonia Hails Its Secession Bid
2014-09-12.abstract: Artur Mas, the president of the regional government, is fighting opposition from Spain’s central government as the Nov. 9 vote approaches.

2014-09-12.title: U.S. Pins Hope on Syrian Rebels With Loyalties All Over the Map
2014-09-12.abstract: President Obama’s determination to train moderate rebels in the battle against ISIS leaves the U.S. dependent on a diverse group riven by infighting.

2014-09-12.title: Try as He May, John McCain Can’t Shake Falsehoods About Ties to ISIS
2014-09-12.abstract: The Arizona Republican has been hounded after a picture of him with members of the Free Syrian Army was misinterpreted and doctored purposely to make it appear that he supported ISIS, which he does not.

2014-09-12.title: Arabs Give Tepid Support to U.S. Fight Against ISIS
2014-09-12.abstract: Three years after the United States left Iraq, its return to the region is getting a less than enthusiastic welcome, with allies finding ways to avoid specific commitments to an expanded effort against Sunni extremists.

2014-09-13.title: Embrace of Social Media Aids Flood Victims in Kashmir
2014-09-13.abstract: Social media companies, government agencies and still-connected individuals have cooperated to help locate some 12,000 people amid the flooding and communications difficulties.

2014-09-13.title: Professor’s Angry Tweets on Gaza Cost Him a Job
2014-09-13.abstract: The University of Illinois rescinded a job offer to a professor over concerns about the tenor of his criticism of Israel’s Gaza offensive on Twitter.

2014-09-13.title: Ex-President of Haiti Put Under House Arrest
2014-09-13.abstract: Jean-Bertrand Aristide was placed on house arrest in a corruption investigation, an order protested by his supporters whom claimed there was no provision in Haitian law for such a penalty.

2014-09-13.title: U.S. Scientists See Long Fight Against Ebola
2014-09-13.abstract: The Ebola outbreak in West Africa is likely to last 12 to 18 months more and could infect hundreds of thousands, say scientists mapping its spread for the American government.

2014-09-13.title: Liberian President Pleads With Obama for Assistance in Combating Ebola
2014-09-13.abstract: Liberia’s president, Ellen Johnson Sirleaf, warned that without American assistance, the Ebola epidemic could send the country into the civil chaos that enveloped it for two decades.

2014-09-14.title: Sun and Wind Alter Global Landscape, Leaving Utilities Behind
2014-09-14.abstract: Electric utility executives all over the world are watching nervously as technologies they once dismissed as irrelevant begin to threaten their long-established business plans.

2014-09-14.title: In Southern China, Residents Wary of the Government Protest a Plan to Burn Waste
2014-09-14.abstract: Residents of a county in the province of Guangdong say they do not trust official reassurances about the environmental safety of a planned incinerator.

2014-09-14.title: Matthew Todd Miller Sentenced to 6 Years of Hard Labor in North Korea
2014-09-14.abstract: The country’s official news agency offered few details of the one-day trial for Mr. Miller, who was convicted of committing “hostile acts” against the North after reportedly tearing up his tourist visa and demanding asylum.

2014-09-14.title: Arab Nations Offer to Fight ISIS From Air
2014-09-14.abstract: American officials have made it clear they do not want the airstrikes to get ahead of the ground action against ISIS, which they said would take time to mass.

2014-09-14.title: 1914: M. Henri Pol Will Go On Caring for Paris Sparrows
2014-09-14.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: An elderly man in Paris assured he would continue looking after sparrows despite the war, in 1914.

2014-09-15.title: Germans Rally to Protest Anti-Semitism Over Gaza War
2014-09-15.abstract: Germany’s Central Council of Jews organized the rally in response to the anti-Semitism seen in cities like Berlin after protesters chanting “death to Israel” and “gas the Jews” at pro-Palestinian demonstrations over the summer had deeply unsettled many.

2014-09-15.abstract: Analysts say Syrian officials are unsure who would benefit militarily if the United States launches strikes against ISIS in Syria — government forces, or Syrian insurgents and separatist Kurds.

2014-09-15.title: In Weddings, Pope Francis Looks Past Tradition
2014-09-15.abstract: In marrying 20 couples, including some who had already lived together, the pope continued to project the image of a more forgiving church.

2014-09-15.title: Libyan Parliament Fires Central Bank Chairman
2014-09-15.abstract: The move by the newly elected Parliament set off a struggle for control of the country’s wealth and vast oil reserves.

2014-09-15.title: Cameron Under Pressure as Scotland Vote Nears
2014-09-15.abstract: Not only must Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain respond to the beheading of a British hostage by Islamic radicals, but he also faces the risk of becoming the leader who presided over the breakup of the United Kingdom.

2014-09-16.title: U.N. Moves Peacekeepers in Golan Over Safety Fears
2014-09-16.abstract: The United Nations said it moved members of its Golan peacekeeping force to the Israeli-controlled side because the area had “deteriorated severely.”

2014-09-16.title: French Premier’s Push Toward Center Opens Rift on the Left
2014-09-16.abstract: Prime Minister Manuel Valls has called a rare confidence vote for Tuesday, as he tries to corral his Socialist Party away from what he calls “old Socialism.”

2014-09-16.title: Kashmiris Cope With Flooding, and Resentment of India
2014-09-16.abstract: A week after disaster struck, many say that aid response by the government is too little, too late.

2014-09-16.title: Loved and Hated, Former Premier of Thailand Is Erased From Textbook
2014-09-16.abstract: In a plugged-in Thailand, it is unclear to what degree the military junta can remove Thaksin Shinawatra from the public’s consciousness.

2014-09-16.abstract: After Mr. Foley was kidnapped by ISIS, the government offered sympathy but little active support, his family said, leaving them overwhelmed and unsure of what to do.

2014-09-17.title: Scottish Independence Vote Balances Politics and Economics
2014-09-17.abstract: A breakdown of the issues voters will consider Thursday in Scotland’s referendum on independence.

2014-09-17.title: Syrian Plane Shot Down as Attacks by Groups Intensify
2014-09-17.abstract: The downing of the Syrian military aircraft was said to be the first such attack by fighters from the Islamic State group.

2014-09-17.title: Health Care for Britain in Harsh Light
2014-09-17.abstract: The decision by a 5-year-old’s parents to take him to the Czech Republic for treatment of a brain tumor has thrown a harsh light on Britain’s National Health Service.

2014-09-17.title: Shiite Militias Pose Challenge for U.S. in Iraq
2014-09-17.abstract: The de facto coalition of militias, the United States and the Iraqi government against the Islamic State shows little promise for stability.

2014-09-17.title: For Miners, Increasing Risk on a Mountain at the Heart of Bolivia’s Identity
2014-09-17.abstract: A silver-rich mountain — seen by many Bolivians as a potent national symbol — is caving in after centuries of mining, but many of the men who work on the mountain do not want to move.

2014-09-18.title: Venezuela: Move to Revoke Actress’s Citizenship
2014-09-18.abstract: The government said Wednesday that it would seek to revoke the citizenship of the actress Maria Conchita Alonso, left, a fervent critic of its socialist-inspired policies.

2014-09-18.title: Rob Ford to Be Treated for Unusual Cancer
2014-09-18.abstract: The Toronto mayor will soon undergo chemotherapy for treatment of two unusual, malignant tumors, a physician said.

2014-09-18.title: A Dutch Town Honors U.S. Soldiers’ Wartime Bravery at Germany’s Edge
2014-09-18.abstract: Nijmegen, the Netherlands, recently completed a showpiece bridge, in memory of a bold daylight crossing of the Waal River by the Americans in World War II.

2014-09-18.title: Kerry on U.S. Strategy Against ISIS
2014-09-18.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry reiterated to lawmakers that there would be no American ground troops in combat against ISIS fighters in Iraq and Syria.

2014-09-18.title: Scotland: Should I Stay or Should I Go?
2014-09-18.abstract: In the week ahead of a historic referendum on Scottish independence, campaigners from both sides took to the streets to sway the vote, which could break a three-century-old union.

2014-09-19.title: Ebola Presents Challenge, and an Opportunity, for U.N. Leader
2014-09-19.abstract: For Ban Ki-moon, in his seventh year leading the United Nations, Ebola represents a crucial test — but also an opportunity to lead the global response in fighting the outbreak.

2014-09-19.title: As Moscow’s Landfills Near Limits, Recyclers Do Whatever It Takes
2014-09-19.abstract: A small but growing movement is working to make it easier to recycle household waste, and advocates say Moscow’s brimming landfills could use the relief.

2014-09-19.title: Fear of Ebola Drives Mob to Kill Officials in Guinea
2014-09-19.abstract: Eight people were killed near a remote village in Guinea after a rock-hurling mob attacked them, claiming they had come to spread the Ebola virus.

2014-09-19.title: Iraq: Deadly Attacks Strike Baghdad
2014-09-19.abstract: At least 29 people were killed and more than 65 wounded in attacks in and around the predominantly Shiite neighborhood of Kadhimiya in Baghdad.

2014-09-19.title: Syria: Opposition Investigates Vaccine
2014-09-19.abstract: Officials of the Syrian opposition coalition said they had created a commission of inquiry into the deaths of children caused by adulterated measles vaccine administered this week.

2014-09-20.title: After Sweep, Australia Adds Security at Parliament
2014-09-20.abstract: The move came a day after 15 people were detained in raids that the prime minister said were based on signs that militant Islamists were planning attacks.

2014-09-20.title: Fleeing Gaza, Only to Face Treachery and Disaster at Sea
2014-09-20.abstract: A Gazan’s son is among the hundreds missing after a migrant ship sank off Malta, and his plight illustrates the risks facing Palestinians seeking illegal passage to Europe.

2014-09-20.title: Sierra Leone’s Aggressive Attack on Ebola
2014-09-20.abstract: Sierra Leone imposed a three-day national lockdown, ordering people off the streets and into their homes in an effort to stamp out the deadly disease.

2014-09-20.title: Hospitals in Kashmir Struggle After Flooding, Deepening a Health Crisis
2014-09-20.abstract: Nearly two weeks after heavy flooding, many hospitals in the region are barely functioning and infectious waters have yet to recede.

2014-09-20.title: Turkish President’s Allies Increasing Pressure on Times and Reporter
2014-09-20.abstract: The New York Times and one of its Istanbul correspondents have been denounced with growing furor in reaction to an article and photograph about the recruitment of fighters in Turkey by the Islamic State.

2014-09-21.title: Paris Falls Out of Love With the Padlocks on Its Bridges
2014-09-21.abstract: Thick glass, with nothing to hitch a lock to, has begun replacing the panels where thousands have symbolized everlasting devotion by fastening hardware.

2014-09-21.title: U.S. Criticizes Efforts to Intimidate Times Reporter in Turkey
2014-09-21.abstract: The correspondent has been attacked online and denounced as a traitor after publication of an article about Islamic State recruiting in Turkey.

2014-09-21.title: After Rancor, Afghans Agree to Share Power
2014-09-21.abstract: After eight months of enmity over the protracted presidential election, Ashraf Ghani and Abdullah Abdullah will have to confront the challenges of jointly governing their country.

2014-09-21.title: 1939: Reich Maps Show France Partitioned, Says Daladier
2014-09-21.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: France accused Germany of partition plans in 1939.

2014-09-21.title: 1964: Otto Grotewohl Dies at 70
2014-09-21.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: The East German prime minister died in 1964.

2014-09-22.title: Spurred by ISIS Violence, Nations Mull How to Press for Justice in Conflicts
2014-09-22.abstract: Western governments set about devising a strategy for speeding prosecution of those responsible for grave crimes in the conflict in Syria and Iraq.

2014-09-22.title: Though Adversaries on the Surface, U.S. and Hezbollah Share a Goal
2014-09-22.abstract: In practice, Hezbollah and the United States are already working — separately — on a common goal: to stop the extremist Islamic State from moving into Lebanon.

2014-09-22.title: For Migrants, a Difficult Journey Through Mexico
2014-09-22.abstract: Mexico is trying to disrupt the flow of migrants traveling from Central America to the United States. Many are determined to make the trip anyway.

2014-09-22.title: Soldiers’ Graves Bear Witness to Russia’s Role in Ukraine
2014-09-22.abstract: Even as the Kremlin tries to deny its involvement and families are pressured to keep quiet, the deaths of soldiers sent to fight in Ukraine are impossible to ignore.

2014-09-22.title: In the Rhine Valley, Clamoring for Relief From Ramped-Up Rail
2014-09-22.abstract: A vital stretch along Europe’s most important north-south rail route is being overrun with train traffic, spoiling the quality of life for residents in a region famous for its natural beauty.

2014-09-23.title: Monitors of Afghan Vote Are Said to Back Secrecy
2014-09-23.abstract: Western officials observing the election pressed for the final numbers to be withheld, as demanded by the losing candidate before he joined a unity government, multiple sources said.

2014-09-23.title: What to Expect From Narendra Modi at the United Nations
2014-09-23.abstract: Since taking power in May, Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India has had runaway success with major speeches in part because of their character – colloquial, earthy and extemporaneous. Will that tone translate to the United Nations General Assembly?

2014-09-23.title: Ukraine’s Neglected and Battered Army Inspires Citizens to Pitch In
2014-09-23.abstract: As casualties have mounted among Ukraine’s outgunned soldiers, citizens and support groups for wounded soldiers have begun to rally behind their army in a rush of patriotic feeling.

2014-09-23.title: Group Backing Islamic State Vows to Kill French Citizen
2014-09-23.abstract: The kidnapping in Algeria by jihadists followed a call by ISIS to begin retaliatory attacks on Westerners.

2014-09-23.title: Fresh Graves Point to Undercount of Ebola Toll
2014-09-23.abstract: While the Sierra Leone Health Ministry has confirmed 10 Ebola deaths, a Freetown cemetery said it had added 110 graves in eight days alone.

2014-09-24.title: A Mayor’s Words Inflame a Debate Over Spanish Attitudes on Women
2014-09-24.abstract: Francisco Javier León de la Riva, the mayor of Valladolid, has faced calls to step down after he said he had qualms about sharing an elevator with a woman because she might falsely claim rape.

2014-09-24.title: Will the U.N. Help Countries Sheltering Syrian Refugees?
2014-09-24.abstract: United Nations agencies such as the World Food Program, Unicef and the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees provide varying levels of assistance to Syrian refugees.

2014-09-24.title: U.S. Is Carrying Out Vast Majority of Strikes on ISIS, Military Officials Say
2014-09-24.abstract: The Obama administration tried to convey a resolute coalition, but the vast majority of attacks were by American warplanes.

2014-09-24.title: Could the U.N. Meet Outside of New York?
2014-09-24.abstract: The General Assembly hall was built in New York in 1952, and this is where the annual high-level sessions have been held every year.

2014-09-24.title: While Syrian Volunteers Seek Support, Crisis Worsens at Home
2014-09-24.abstract: Members of the Syria Civil Defense, an opposition group, traveled to the United Nations to seek support. But their minds were on colleagues at home aiding those they say were injured in airstrikes targeting militants.

2014-09-25.title: Hassan Rouhani, President of Iran, Experiences Rare Unscripted Moment
2014-09-25.abstract: President Rouhani was giving a speech that was suddenly punctuated with an outburst by spectators concerned about political prisoners in Iran.

2014-09-25.title: Leaders’ Speeches at U.N. Show Delicacy of Mission Against ISIS Militants
2014-09-25.abstract: Remarks by leaders on Day 1 of the General Assembly hinted at the narrow, often competing, interests of some of America’s allies in the fight against terrorism.

2014-09-25.title: To Tuscan Wine Makers, a Preservation Plan Takes On Acrid Undertones
2014-09-25.abstract: Tuscany’s regional government recently approved a new rural preservation plan, saying that too many vines had been planted and warned of the risk of soil erosion.

2014-09-25.title: In Israel, Values of a Holy Respite Are Adapted for a High-Tech World
2014-09-25.abstract: Israel experiments with modern observances of the Jewish “shmita,” a traditional rest period for farmers that comes every seventh year.

2014-09-25.title: Treasury Imposes Terrorism Sanctions
2014-09-25.abstract: The Treasury Department announced sanctions on 11 individuals and one organization it said helped finance the flow of fighters for terrorist groups.

2014-09-26.title: Woman Hides Camera to Reveal Life Under Islamic State Rule
2014-09-26.abstract: A woman in Raqqa, Syria, documented life under Islamic State rule by concealing a camera in the folds of the cloak she is compelled to wear.

2014-09-26.title: In Chile, an Animal Whose Numbers Please No One
2014-09-26.abstract: Government-sanctioned culling of the guanaco in Tierra del Fuego has drawn opposition from animal rights activists and tourism officials.

2014-09-26.title: Francis Removes Bishop Under Cloud in Paraguay
2014-09-26.abstract: Some see the pope meting out discipline after an era plagued by misconduct and cover-ups, though the target of the dismissal protested the handling of related circumstances.

2014-09-26.title: Clashing Goals in Syria Strikes Bedevil Obama
2014-09-26.abstract: On the third day of American-led airstrikes in Syria, it was increasingly uncertain whether the United States could punish terrorist groups without aiding President Bashar al-Assad.

2014-09-26.title: Militant in Beheading Videos Has Been Identified, F.B.I. Chief Says
2014-09-26.abstract: James B. Comey, director of the F.B.I., declined to name the man believed to be the Islamic State militant in the videos while agents search for him.

2014-09-27.title: Mexico Holds 8 in Army in Inquiry of 22 Deaths
2014-09-27.abstract: The arrests represent a reversal for officials who initially described the June encounter as a shootout.

2014-09-27.title: U.S. Considers a No-Fly Zone to Protect Civilians From Airstrikes by Syria
2014-09-27.abstract: The administration is open to establishing a buffer zone along the Turkish-Syrian border, where tens of thousands of Syrians have sought refuge.

2014-09-27.title: A Greek Politician Willing to Face the People
2014-09-27.abstract: Rena Dourou’s election as prefect of Athens represents the biggest victory so far for the left-wing Syriza Party.

2014-09-27.title: Raising Hands to Join Peacekeeping Forces
2014-09-27.abstract: Mexico, Indonesia, Mongolia and Bangladesh pledge greater participation, and Japan says it will change its laws so it can send troops.

2014-09-27.title: Beatification Brings Opus Dei Into the Open
2014-09-27.abstract: The beatification Mass for a former leader of Opus Dei is an unusually public show of force for the movement, one of the most powerful and discreet in Catholicism.

2014-09-28.title: Advocates for News Media to Meet Turkish Officials
2014-09-28.abstract: Two advocacy groups left for Turkey to meet with the officials, who may include President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

2014-09-28.title:
Private Bank Fuels
Fortunes of Putin’s
Inner Circle
2014-09-28.abstract: The rise of Bank Rossiya, controlled by close friends of President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, illustrates how he has enriched loyalists whose influence over the economy has helped him maintain his iron grip on power.

2014-09-28.title: 1914: German Offensive Checked in Belgium
2014-09-28.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: German troops are surprised in Belgium in 1914.

2014-09-28.title: Crackdown on Protests by Hong Kong Police Draws More to the Streets
2014-09-28.abstract: Downtown Hong Kong turned into a battlefield of tear gas and seething crowds on Sunday after the police moved against a student democracy protest.

2014-09-28.title: Dozens of Hikers Feared Dead on Japanese Volcano
2014-09-28.abstract: Thirty-one climbers were feared dead on Mount Ontake, which had been busy with hikers who had gone to see the first signs of autumn.

2014-09-29.title: Taliban Press for Advantage as Politicians Work on Maneuvers in Kabul
2014-09-29.abstract: A brutal battle in Ghazni Province was the latest sign that the insurgency was becoming increasingly ambitious, posing the central challenge facing the country’s new president.

2014-09-29.title: Iraq Army Woos Deserters Back to War on ISIS
2014-09-29.abstract: The Iraqi military command has launched a campaign to re-enlist soldiers and officers who abandoned their units.

2014-09-29.title: You Call This Thai Food? The Robotic Taster Will Be the Judge
2014-09-29.abstract: A boxy contraption scans food samples to produce a chemical signature, which it measures against a standard deemed to be that of authentic Thai food.

2014-09-29.title: Training to Fight ISIS
2014-09-29.abstract: The Iraqi military command has launched a campaign to re-enlist soldiers and officers who abandoned their units.

2014-09-29.title: Nusra Front Vows Retaliation for Strikes in Syria
2014-09-29.abstract: The leader of the Syrian rebel group and Qaeda offshoot threatens America in a YouTube video, a shift from the group’s prior focus on ousting Bashar al-Assad.

2014-09-30.title: As Catholic Church Seeks Proof, Venezuela Sees a Saint
2014-09-30.abstract: The Roman Catholic Church is seeking evidence of miracles linked to Dr. José Gregorio Hernández, who died in 1919.

2014-09-30.title: The Hague: Bosnian’s Trial Nears End
2014-09-30.abstract: Prosecutors on Monday demanded a life sentence for the former Bosnian leader Radovan Karadzic as his trial draws to a close.

2014-09-30.title: Mexico: Students Missing After Protest
2014-09-30.abstract: Authorities in Guerrero are searching for 57 students from a teachers college who have been missing since Friday when gunmen fired on a demonstration they were attending.

2014-09-30.title: Opposition in Syria Is Skeptical of U.S. Airstrikes on ISIS
2014-09-30.abstract: Protests against American airstrikes have broken out across insurgent-held Syria over the past week, including among some of the very insurgents who spent years calling for them.

2014-09-30.title: Many Missteps in Assessment of ISIS Threat
2014-09-30.abstract: The question of how officials failed to anticipate the rise of ISIS resonates inside and outside the Obama administration.
